LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT JERRY OSTRIN'S DEMURRER TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ALLEGED AGAINST HIM IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BUT WITH PERMISSION TO REFILE THOSE CLAIMS IN THE FAMILY LAW CASE BETWEEN MS. ZOLFAGHARI AND MR. OSTRIN. ALL CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ALLEGED AGAINST MR. OSTRIN ARE STAYED PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE FAMILY LAW CASE. A THRESHOLD ISSUE AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ALLEGED AGAINST MR. OSTRIN IS WHETHER MR. OSTRIN HAS AN OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN PHOENIX RISING ENTERPRISES, INC. PER PARAGRAPH 5 OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, THAT IS ONE OF THE ISSUES IN THE FAMILY LAW CASE. TO AVOID THE POSSIBILITY OF INCONSISTENT RULINGS BETWEEN THIS CASE AND THE FAMILY LAW CASE AND BECAUSE CLAIMS BETWEEN FORMER SPOUSES ARE MORE PROPERLY ADJUDICATED IN FAMILY COURT RATHER THAN BY WAY OF A CIVIL ACTION, ALL CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ALLEGED AGAINST MR. OSTRIN ARE STAYED PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE FAMILY LAW CASE. IF ANY OF THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION HAVE NOT BEEN FULLY ADJUDICATED IN THE FAMILY LAW CASE, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS MAY REQUEST THAT THE STAY BE LIFTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment; Declaration Of Frank A. Cialone
Off Calendar May-25-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT JERRY OSTRIN's Motion For Entry Of Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Cross-Defendant Jerry Ostrin's motion for entry of judgment is granted. By order filed December 15, 2016, the court sustained Mr. Ostrin's demurrer to the cross-complaint without leave to amend, but with permission to refile those claims in the family law case between Ms. Zolfaghari and Mr. Ostrin [In re Marriage of Jerry Ostrin and Kathia Zolfaghari, Case No. FDI-15-784382], and stayed all causes of action in the cross-complaint alleged against Mr. Ostrin pending the conclusion of the family law case. The order provided that if any of the causes of action of the cross-complaint have not been fully adjudicated in the family law case, cross-complainants may request that the stay be lifted. Thereafter, on March 29, 2021, the family law court entered an order granting Mr. Ostrin's motion for determination of good faith settlement and judgment on the pleadings in the instant action. That court found that Mr. Ostrin's settlement with Cross-Complainant Katherine Walker was made in good faith within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6, and that its order "shall bar any person, including Zolfaghari and PRE, from any claims against Ostrin for contribution, indemnity, or other liability that arises out of or relates in any way to the claims asserted by Walker. Accordingly, Ostrin is entitled to judgment on the pleadings in the Walker Litigation, and the Cross-Complaint against him shall be dismissed with prejudice." Defendants and Cross-Complainants' late-filed and mistitled opposition misstates the legal effect of a good faith determination under section 877.6 and of the family court's order, which is dispositive.

Order signed. Separate judgment signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
Off Calendar Feb-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Gonzalez, Esq. 805-576-7776 of Gonzalez Law Group for Defendant Phoenix Rising Enterprise.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT JERRY OSTRIN's MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.

Cross-defendant Ostrin's motion for determination of good faith settlement and judgment on the pleadings is denied without prejudice. First, this action is stayed, and movant has not sought to lift the stay. It appears that the motion should be filed in the family court action. Second, cross-defendant fails to present any evidence that would enable the Court to determine "a rough approximation of plaintiffs' total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v., Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) "If 'there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion.'" (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348 [settling parties did not proffer any evidence regarding their proportionate liability for plaintiff's alleged injury, but merely presented a series of "questionable assumptions" in their memorandum to show the settlement amount was reasonable].)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Thomas E. Duckworth and J. Erik Heath of Duckworth Peters Lebowitz Olivier LLP (415.433.0333) appear as counsels for plaintiff/cross-defendant/moving party, Katherine Walker

Attorney, Joseph Gonzalez of The Bloom Firm (818.914.7397) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant, Phoenix Rising Enterprises

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS PHOENIX RISING ENTERPRISES, INC., KATHIA ZOLFAGHARI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED in its entirety. The court grants the request to take judicial notice of the declaration of Mr. Ostrin. The court can take judicial notice of the declaration as a court record, but it cannot take judicial notice of the truth of the matters asserted therein. (People v. Tolbert (1986) 176 Cal.App.3d 685, 690.) The ownership issue is disputed and therefore, not a subject for judicial notice. The first amended complaint alleges that Phoenix Rising Enterprises is a corporation and is a distinct entity from its owners. The face of the first amended complaint and judicially noticeable documents do not show that Mr. Ostrin is an indispensable party. Ms. Walker has adequately alleged all of her claims. The Labor Code claims need not be alleged with particularity. Defendants' argument that Ms. Walker's alleged harm was somehow self-inflicted because she had the title of chief operating officer and determined working conditions fails because it is directly contradictory to the well-pleaded allegations that defendants controlled all aspects of Ms. Walker's employment. (FAC pars. 3 and 6-7.). Defendants also improperly attempt to introduce extrinsic facts that are not alleged in the first amended complaint. Defendants cite no authority for the proposition that they have no liability on any of Ms. Walker's claims because Ms. Walker was a corporate officer. The court takes plaintiff's allegations at par. 40 that male employees were treated differently than female employees as true. As to the fifth cause of action, paragraph 48 of the first amended complaint alleges that the discrimination/harassment was because plaintiff did not share Ms. Zolfaghel's religious viewpoints. As to the sixth and seventh causes of action, defendants ignore the allegations in the first amended complaint that allege that Ms. Walker was an employee (FAC pars. 3 and 6-7) and defendants had the requisite number of employees to implicate FEHA (FAC par. 47). Defendants' demurrer to the "rape" and "fairy" language and the claim for punitive damages is procedurally improper. A party may demurrer to a cause of action, not particular words or a prayer for relief. A motion to strike is the vehicle to attack irrelevant, false, or improper words or prayers. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Thomas E. Duckworth and J. Erik Heath of Duckworth Peters Lebowitz Olivier LLP (415.433.0333) appear as counsels for plaintiff/cross-defendant/moving party, Katherine Walker

Attorney, Joseph Gonzalez of The Bloom Firm (818.914.7397) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-complainant, Phoenix Rising Enterprises

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS DEFENDANT KATHERINE WALKER'S Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint is DENIED because Ms. Walker failed to meet her burden on the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis showing that the cross-complaint or any claims therein arise from petitioning activity. Instead the record shows that the cross-complaint is based on Ms. Walker's alleged failure to perform her executive duties and how that failure caused cross-complainants' damages. City of Cotati v. Cashman (2002) 29 Cal.4th 69is the controlling case. Lennar Homes of California, Inc. v. Stephens (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 673 is distinguishable for two reasons. First, Lennar did not dispute the key issue here, and accordingly, the question of whether Lennar's action "arose from" the federal lawsuit or an underlying dispute was not discussed. "Opinions are not authority for issues they do not consider." (Melchor Investment Co. v. Rolm Systems (1992) 3 Cal.App.4th 587, 591.) Second, Lennar's action was not a cross-complaint. If Ms. Walker's argument is taken to its logical conclusion, every cross-complaint or at least every cross-complaint for indemnity is a SLAPP because but for the filing of Ms. Walker's complaint, the cross-complaint would not have been filed. This is true of every cross-complaint, and it reflects the type of simplistic analysis the California Supreme Court warned courts to avoid. "To construe 'arising from' in section 425.16, subdivision (b)(1) as meaning 'in response to,' ... would in effect render all cross-actions potential SLAPP's." (City of Cotati at 77.) In deciding the first prong, the focus is on the gravamen of the cross-claims to determine whether the cross-complaint is a SLAPP. "Determining the gravamen of the claims requires examination of the specific acts of alleged wrongdoing and not just the form of the claim." (Bergstein v. Stroock & Stroock & Lavan LLP (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 793, 804.) In this case, there is an underlying disagreement whether Ms. Walker's discharged her executive duties, whether she caused harm to the defendants, and whether defendants should be indemnified for the alleged non/misfeasance. Cross-complainants are seeking to base liability on Walker's dereliction of duty, not her petitioning activity. Cross-complainants' request for fees is denied. Although lacking in merit, the motion was not frivolous. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
